title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. borreri
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. borreri, also known as Borrer's male fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris filix-mas, a type of fern native to Europe and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to act as a natural antiseptic for wounds and skin irritations. Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. borreri can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
